text: 80.7g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0.7g of carbs. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. not relevant here.
0.2g of protein. the rest of the section is nothing special. also not relevant here.
the rest of the section is nothing special.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

the upsides first: easy and convenient to use because simple practical upside. often inexpensive because a practical reason it still exists in real kitchens. very familiar product because people know what it is and how to use it.

on the weaker side: processing is a major category negative because this is the core reason margarine makes a clean weak anchor in this category. ingredient simplicity is poor because much weaker trust and wholefood fit than stronger oils/fats anchors. quality varies wildly by formulation because that makes the category fit feel unstable and less trustworthy.
D tier. Best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
